description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with two stages: test and pages. In the test stage add a test-linux job that uses the image registry.gitlab.com/jangorecki/dockerfiles/r-base-dev, runs on Linux runners, executes a before_script `echo \"Revision:\" $CI_BUILD_REF >> ./DESCRIPTION`, then runs `R CMD build .`, `R CMD check $(ls -1t *.tar.gz | head -n 1) --as-cran --no-manual`, creates `bus/src/contrib`, moves the built tar.gz into it, and runs `Rscript -e 'tools::write_PACKAGES(contrib.url(\"bus\"), fields=\"Revision\", addFiles=TRUE)'`; it always stores `r.gitlab.ci.Rcheck` and `bus` as artifacts for 4 weeks. Also add a test-windows job that runs on Windows runners, performs a before_script that downloads and silently installs R 4.0.0 and Rtools40 via `curl.exe` and `Start-Process`, updates `PATH`, and appends the revision to `DESCRIPTION`, then runs `R.exe CMD build .`, `R.exe CMD check $(ls.exe -1t *.tar.gz | head -n 1) --as-cran --no-manual`, `R.exe CMD INSTALL $(ls.exe -1t *.tar.gz | head -n 1) --build`, creates `bus/bin/windows/contrib/4.0`, moves the built zip there, and runs `Rscript.exe -e \"tools::write_PACKAGES(contrib.url('bus', type='win.binary'), type='win.binary', fields='Revision', addFiles=TRUE)\"`; it also always stores `r.gitlab.ci.Rcheck` and `bus` as artifacts for 4 weeks. In the pages stage add a pages job that uses the ubuntu image, runs on Linux tags, only on the master branch, depends on test-linux and test-windows, creates a `public` directory, moves everything from `bus/*` into `public`, and publishes the `public/` directory as a GitLab Pages artifact with a 4‑week expiration.
